name: Northern Territory (Australia)

colors.symbolism: the territorial colour at the hoist
the ochre colour of the territorial fly, evoking the desert and the soil
the stars of the Southern Cross and the petals of the desert rose

description: A vertical bicolour with a black panel at the hoist (one third) bearing the white stars of the Southern Cross, and an ochre panel at the fly (two thirds) bearing a stylised Sturt's desert rose with seven white petals around a black seven-pointed star.
history: Adopted on 1 July 1978, the date the Northern Territory achieved self-government. Designed by artist Robert Ingpen, who was commissioned by the territorial government to create a flag using the official colours of black, white and ochre.

features.symbolism: black and ochre are the official territorial colours, with white added; the colours represent the Outback landscape and identity of the Territory

features.role: the Southern Cross constellation on the black panel
features.symbolism: the constellation Crux, visible in the southern sky

features.role: stylised Sturt's desert rose with seven petals on the ochre panel
features.symbolism: Sturt's desert rose, the floral emblem of the Northern Territory; its seven petals surround a black seven-pointed star representing the six Australian states and the Territory itself

features.role: seven-pointed star at the centre of the desert rose
features.symbolism: the six Australian states and the Northern Territory united in the Commonwealth
